TRANSCRIPT
Book Launch Bonus – PRELAUNCH PART 1
TRANSCRIPT
Let's talk about your Prelaunch. First of all for a book, as I mentioned already, doing
three videos that leads up to the sale of a book is a bit of overkill. Really, your Prelaunch
ends up being a lot more similar to a Pre-Prelaunch in Product Launch Formula
terms.
Really, it's about letting your people know that something's coming, letting them know
that you've got a book coming. Unless you've got dozens and dozens of books out
there, releasing a book is something special. It's a big deal. That was one of the things
that I didn't realize coming into my launch, just how big a deal it was, especially for my first
book. There was a lot of pent up demand for that book, that was another big asset I had, there
was a lot of pent up demand. To get anything from me, a Product Launch is for less than two
thousand dollars, you just couldn't do it. Here people are going to be able to buy a book from
me, so that was really huge.
I also underestimated how much my partners were interested in sharing about the book
because when they went and told their lists about the book, it really was a high value offer for
their book instead of just trying to get their list to buy something, buy another thing, a book is
seen as different. It's almost magical. It's really pretty interesting. That's why this whole Pre-
Prelaunch, or your Prelaunch, whichever you want to call it, I really think of it more like a Pre-
Pre, it's really just about letting your people…when I say your people I mean your list, whether
that's your email list or your social media, following your client list, whatever. Whatever your
entire platform is, letting them know that something's coming.
It's often about co-creation. That's what a Pre-Prelaunch is typically
about, it's that shot across the bow. Something's coming. This is really cool.
You're telling them something's coming without actually selling them. It is often
about co-creation, that's why if you go back and study the Pre-Pre in module form, in Product
Launch Formula, you'll see that one of the tools I often use is a survey. The survey could be,
"What do I need to include in this course?" You're going to see in just a minute how I used a
survey in my book launch.
It's often about co-creation because people support that which they help create. If you
can get people to help create the book or feel like they've helped in the creation process in even
the smallest way, then they're going to tend to support you more.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

!3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
TRANSCRIPT
Book Launch Bonus – PRELAUNCH PART 1
Think about how you can get your people to help your book promotion. How can you
leverage their influence? That's the key. Unless you have just a huge, huge, enormous list, then
just getting your people to buy a book isn't going to move the needle as much as getting them to
try to help spread your influence through their social media following, through word of mouth,
telling their friends, telling other people that have a following and have a list. That's really what
it's about, thinking of your clients, your list, your platform, as being this inner circle, and how
can you get that inner circle to help you try to create more influence.
You want to use the standard PLF Mental Triggers from Module 2 and
definitely go review the Pre-Pre and the Prelaunch material in Module 4. For my
book launch I just want to show you how I started this off with. I did start it with a survey but it
was a little bit of a different survey.
For the first part of my Prelaunch, or my Pre-Prelaunch really, I put together a design
contest at 99 Designs. 99 Designs, you can see the website right here, it's a great place to get
designs done for logos or any graphical type of thing you want to do. In this case I did it for my
book cover. I did it because I wanted to have a great book cover. What you can do, you can go
there and you can create a contest and, basically, your designers will compete and they'll create a
lot of different designs and then you pick the best design, and the best design is the contest
winner and they get paid. You decide how much money you want to put up. It could be two or
three hundred dollars, I actually put up more for this because I had the budget and I wanted to
really attract a lot of designers, but I had dozens and dozens of designers competing and they
created over 500 different designs for me. Basically, you can pick them, you can give them
feedback, they can make iterations, they can make changes, and then eventually you pick the
winner.
None of that, of course, is part of my Pre-Prelaunch, I'm just giving you that FYI. Then
what you can do is you can create a survey or poll within your design and have people look at
your design and give you feedback. What I did was, as the contest was drawing to the
end and I had an idea of my top designs, then I created a poll, it's all automatic
within 99 Designs, then I sent that poll or that survey out to my list asking them
which one they wanted. That was my shot across the bow.
Here you can see the email I sent out. I think the subject for this email was "Please help
me pick my book cover” - something along that line. You can see the copy here is, "So the big
news is my first bookstore book is almost done. There's just a few finishing touches before I send
it to the publisher. But first I need to pick a cover for the book. I need your help. Can you tell me
which cover you like best? Just click this link:…”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
TRANSCRIPT
Book Launch Bonus – PRELAUNCH PART 1
If you look at this copy and the way I did this, it's classic Pre-Prelaunch. I'm letting them
know something's coming. I'm not trying to sell them anything. I'm telling them there's just a
little bit to go and I need your feedback. I'm looking to get them invested. It's only going to take
you a minute, and if you want you can leave me comments. When people clicked on that link,
they went over to this contest. You can see this copy up here was 581 entries. "Jeff Walker is
running a book or magazine cover contest. So far, designers have submitted 581 entries and Jeff
Walker would love your feedback on the four entries below."
This is all automatically created for me by 99 Designs. There's zero work involved. I set
up that contest at 99 Designs, which is real easy to do. I put up my money and then the contest
went along. I will warn you, it's quite a bit of work because, to get the best results you end up
giving people a lot of feedback, and the whole giving feedback can take a lot of time, but you've
got a lot of different designs. You can see here we ran four very, very different designs for the
book cover - radically different designs.
Another thing I did was I actually did multiple contests. I did one contest for my
platinum mastermind group, I did one contest for another mastermind I'm in, I did another
contest for my PLF owners, I did another contest for my prospects just because I wanted to
test…I really did want the feedback on the book covers and I kept on iterating and making
changes. You can see this is the one that eventually won right here, even though that's not
exactly what we ended up with, so people could come in here, they could pick their rating, and
then they could leave a comment.
The back end, what I saw, the results were you could see this one won, this ended up
being our cover, or very, very similar to the cover. We ended up changing that asterisk there, the
exclamation point there, ended up being turned into an upward facing arrow. I think we ended
up with a stronger design that way, but you can see here, this one got 382 four-star ratings. The
next one got 213 ratings, 184 four-star ratings and so on. Plus people could leave comments.
This did a bunch of different things. I ended up with a really nice book cover. I ended up with
this poll or this survey, finding out what people thought was the best.
I also got these great comments and there's a lot of gold in these comments. It was really
cool, but the thing is, part of the Pre-Prelaunch, it just lets people know something’s coming.
Remember that idea that people support that which they help create. This may
not seem like a big deal, but by helping pick the book cover, they helped in the
creation of the book. That just helps them get invested. Basically, with this I was able
to do a lot of cool things, get great feedback on the book covers and get people invested and let
them know something was coming. That was the first step of my Pre-Prelaunch.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
